pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room but then i remember. everything just feels so heavy and empty... i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. it’s like i’m just a ghost wandering around a house that doesn't belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how tiny everything is. like, the universe is just expanding into nothingness and in a few billion years there wont be a single trace that humans ever even existed. it makes everything i do feel so pointless. why even bother trying to build anything or be anything if it's all just gonna be erased anyway?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still bothering. the lab results came back and it's just... everything is gone. the funding, the thesis, the last five years. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this work and now that it's dead, i'm just empty. honestly i just want the noise to stop. i just want to sleep and never have to feel this heavy again.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the heavy, aching pulse in my very soul. He is gone... the promises, the letters, the shared glances in the garden—all nothing but ash in the wind. I find myself staring into the shadows of this lonely chamber, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when the world has become such a hollow, cruel place. There is no color left in the dawn, only a grey, crushing weight. I feel as though I am already a ghost, haunting the ruins of a life that no longer belongs to me. Is there any dignity left in enduring such a betrayal, or is it more noble to simply let the darkness take what remains?

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here in this cafe and realize that if i just stopped showing up... if i just vanished tomorrow... no one in this city would even notice. like i'm just a ghost passing through. it's like i don't even actually exist.

pool.baseline: i blew it all. everything. the company is gone, the investors are suing, and i'm staring at more debt than i could pay off in three lifetimes. i can't even think about going back home. the look on my parents faces... the shame is just too much. i feel like i've reached the end of the road and there's nowhere left to go.
